briefs.target_keyword: how to stop freezing in interviews
briefs.angle: The psychology of why people freeze under pressure — and a three-step rehearsal protocol to stop it
briefs.toneNotes: Empathetic, research-backed, action-oriented. Lead with the Yerkes-Dodson curve. CIT-010 is a natural fit but check rotation.

briefs.target_keyword: is using AI during an interview cheating
briefs.angle: Honest answer: yes. Here is what the research says about skill-building vs. performance crutches.
briefs.toneNotes: Confident, ethical positioning. Reference CodeSignal 2025 fraud report and Roy Lee/NBC News case. Banned words 'real-time' and 'undetectable' may appear in critical framing only.

briefs.target_keyword: how to answer interview questions without rambling
briefs.angle: Rambling is a structure problem, not a confidence problem. The STAR method solves it.
briefs.toneNotes: Practical, diagnostic. Readers self-identify as ramblers — meet them where they are.

briefs.angle: Anxiety is preparation in disguise. What cognitive science says about reappraising pre-performance stress.
briefs.toneNotes: Reassuring but evidence-based. CIT-007 (Brooks 2014 anxiety reappraisal) is the ideal primary — check rotation.

briefs.angle: Three complete STAR answers at different experience levels — entry, mid, senior — for the same behavioral question.
briefs.toneNotes: Concrete and specific. Readers are looking for templates they can adapt immediately. Supporting article under /star-method-guide pillar — do NOT cover head term 'STAR method' directly.
